title: ERRO[0002] could not find slirp4netns
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

ERRO[0002] could not find slirp4netns, the network namespace won't be configured: exec: "slirp4netns": executable file not found in $PATH

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Add Podman Ubuntu repository

2. Install Podman using offical method

3. Start a container

**Describe the results you received:**

No network...

**Describe the results you expected:**

Functional network...

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.16.1
  cgroupManager: cgroupfs
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon'
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.20, commit: '
  cpus: 12
  distribution:
    distribution: linuxmint
    version: "20"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: mobichris
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.4.0-48-generic
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 2041085952
  memTotal: 33548574720
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'runc: /usr/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 1019998208
  swapTotal: 1023406080
  uptime: 3h 26m 45.82s (Approximately 0.12 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
  - docker-dev.repo.expretio.com
store:
  configFile: /home/cbourque/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: vfs
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /home/cbourque/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus: {}
  imageStore:
    number: 3
  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers
  volumePath: /home/cbourque/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 2.0.0
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Wed Dec 31 19:00:00 1969
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.15.2
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 2.1.1
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 2.1.1~1 amd64 [installed]
podman/unknown 2.1.1~1 arm64
podman/unknown 2.1.1~1 armhf
podman/unknown 2.1.1~1 s390x
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical / Linux Mint 20 (Ubuntu 20.04)

The workaround is to manually install the missing package but IMHO it should be a mandatory dependency of the podman installation:
```
sudo apt install slirp4netns
```

comments.body: We've gone back and forth on this one before; there are reasons to not want to install it by default (there were issues filed when it was a hard dependency requesting that we return it to a Recommends, but I can't seem to locate them). I believe it's set as a Recommended dependency now - @lsm5 can you confirm?
@mheon ok but what are the other options?
This is an Ubuntu packaging issue.  It is a Recommends in Fedora and should be similar in Ubuntu.

@rhatdan But how can this be a "recommended" dependency when this network mode is the default in Podman?

```
--network string                           Connect a container to a network (default "slirp4netns")
```
It is only required for rootless Podman. Running as root does not require `slirp4netns` (but does require the CNI plugins)

It shows up as a "recommends" here, so should be installed by default (unless using --no-install-recommends)

```
Depends: libseccomp2, libdevmapper1.02.1, libgpgme11, catatonit, conmon (>= 2.0.18~1), containers-common (>= 1.1.1~2), containernetworking-plugins (>= 0.8.6~1), runc | cri-o-runc, iptables, podman-plugins
Recommends: crun, slirp4netns, uidmap, varlink
Conflicts: podman-rootless
```

```console
$ sudo apt install podman
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
The following additional packages will be installed:
  catatonit conmon containernetworking-plugins containers-common containers-golang containers-image crun podman-plugins
  slirp4netns uidmap varlink
The following NEW packages will be installed:
  catatonit conmon containernetworking-plugins containers-common containers-golang containers-image crun podman
  podman-plugins slirp4netns uidmap varlink
0 upgraded, 12 newly installed, 0 to remove and 2 not upgraded.
Need to get 11,3 MB/27,9 MB of archives.
After this operation, 131 MB of additional disk space will be used.
Do you want to continue? [Y/n]
```

It is not required, since `sudo podman` should work without it. But it also should have been installed by default ?
@ocafebabe I believe everything is correct on Ubuntu, and this was a local install problem.  Reopen if you disagree.

@rhatdan I guess Linux Mint behaves differently than Ubuntu when installing podman, even if it's a derivative of the later. It's also odd that this dependency is recommended on Ubuntu but required on these distributions: Gentoo, Arch Linux, openSUSE Tumbleweed...

It is recommended in RHEL, Centos and Fedora as well.
The key issue is fuse-overlay, slirp4netns are only required for rootless.  Each distro has the ability to define their own specification.  Personally I prefer Recommended, just so I can get rid of packages that I don't need, especially when embedding tools within containers.
